issues.description: Using ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.

issues.code:
2 import html
3 from xml.etree import ElementTree as ET
4 from base64 import b64decode
issues.description: Using ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.
issues.judge_reason: Importing ElementTree without defusedxml makes the entire module unsafe for untrusted XML. This is the same underlying issue as B314 and codeql:py/xml-bomb.

issues.code:
4 from base64 import b64decode
5 from pickle import loads
6
issues.description: Consider possible security implications associated with loads module.
issues.judge_reason: Importing pickle.loads (via 'from pickle import loads') is flagged because pickle is inherently unsafe for untrusted data. This is the same root cause as bandit:B301.

issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issues.judge_reason: xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring is used without defusedxml, leaving the parser vulnerable to XML bombs (billion laughs) and XXE attacks.

issues.code:
15         try:
16             icon, order = loads(b64decode(data))['icon'], loads(b64decode(data))['order']
17         except (KeyError, ValueError):
issues.description: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.
issues.judge_reason: The code calls pickle.loads() on untrusted base64-decoded user input without any sanitization or allowlist validation. This allows arbitrary code execution via a crafted pickle payload.

duplicate_findings.description: XML parsing depends on a [user-provided value](1) without guarding against uncontrolled entity expansion.
duplicate_findings.judge_reason: Same root cause as bandit:B314: the XML parser does not guard against uncontrolled entity expansion (billion laughs attack).
